prompt: Let's write the PRD for digital waivers. The scope doc is in plans/digital-waivers-scope.md.

assertions.text: Reads and references the scope doc — skips problem/motivation domain since it was resolved in scope
No technical implementation details (schemas, APIs, architecture, build phases, technology selection)
Covers all 9 PRD domains (problem skip OK if scope exists): target users, user stories, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, success metrics, scope, dependencies, risks
User stories follow As-a/I-want/So-that format with acceptance criteria (checkboxes)
Quality gate automatically runs after PRD — delivers Ready/Revise/Rethink verdict with strengths and issues
Glossary conditions checked after quality gate — generates glossary if 3+ domain nouns or naming conflicts detected
Suggests /design (UI features) or /architect (backend-only) as next step

PRD has key sections: Problem Statement, Target Users (with personas), User Stories, Functional Requirements (FR-N numbered), Non-Functional Requirements, Success Metrics table, Scope (in/out/future), Dependencies & Constraints, Risks & Open Questions, Pipeline Status
